name: Clark-Fulton Community Development Collaborative
description: Community-based organization hosting asset mapping and neighborhood development efforts.
source_text: Hosted by: Clark-Fulton Community Development Collaborative

name: La Sagrada Familia Parish
description: Faith-based institution providing gathering space and hosting community meetings.
source_text: thanked Father Ignacio and the parish for hosting, and acknowledged that the building has held neighborhood gatherings for over 40 years

name: La Sagrada Familia Parish Hall
description: Physical gathering space with 40+ years of history hosting neighborhood events.
source_text: the building has held neighborhood gatherings for over 40 years

name: Denise Okafor
description: Community facilitator with expertise in asset-based community development and facilitation.
source_text: Facilitated by: Denise Okafor (Lead)

name: Marcus Webb
description: Co-facilitator and documentation specialist supporting community asset mapping.
source_text: Facilitated by: Denise Okafor (Lead) and Marcus Webb (Co-facilitator / Scribe)

name: Multilingual Community Member
description: Resident with fluency in English, Spanish, and Arabic providing translation services and communication skills.
source_text: I speak English, Spanish, and a little Arabic — came in handy during COVID

name: Plumber — Community Member
description: Skilled plumber providing informal maintenance and repair services to neighbors.
source_text: Plumbing. I've fixed sinks in half this block.

name: Plumbing Services — Neighborhood Skill
description: Local plumbing expertise and informal service provision by a neighborhood resident.
source_text: Plumbing. I've fixed sinks in half this block.

name: Baker — Community Member
description: Resident baker who provides food for neighbors during life transitions including births and deaths.
source_text: I bake. I bring food to anyone who just had a baby or lost someone.

name: Baking and Food Provision
description: Local baking skill and food provision that supports community care and connection.
source_text: I bake. I bring food to anyone who just had a baby or lost someone.

name: Neighborhood Historian
description: Long-term resident with deep knowledge of Clark-Fulton history dating back to the 1960s.
source_text: I know this neighborhood's history back to the 1960s. I remember when Fulton Road was all Czech businesses.

name: Neighborhood History and Czech Heritage
description: Collective memory and heritage of Czech business history on Fulton Road maintained through long-term residents.
source_text: I know this neighborhood's history back to the 1960s. I remember when Fulton Road was all Czech businesses.

name: Youth Flag Football League
description: Informal youth sports program led by a community member on volunteer basis.
source_text: I run a youth flag football league — unpaid

name: Nurse — Community Member
description: Healthcare professional employed at MetroHealth providing nursing expertise and availability to the neighborhood.
source_text: Nursing. Night shifts at MetroHealth but I'm here on my off days.

name: MetroHealth
description: Healthcare institution providing employment and professional resources within reach of Clark-Fulton residents.
source_text: Night shifts at MetroHealth but I'm here on my off days.

name: Mechanic — Community Member
description: Skilled auto mechanic providing informal car repair services to neighbors.
source_text: I fix cars. I don't advertise but my neighbors know.

name: Auto Repair Services
description: Informal mechanical repair service available within the neighborhood.
source_text: I fix cars. I don't advertise but my neighbors know.

name: Recovery Mentor — Community Member
description: Long-term recovery advocate (11+ years sober) helping neighbors in substance recovery.
source_text: I've been sober 11 years and I help people find their way in.

name: Recovery Support Network
description: Informal peer support and mentoring network for people in recovery.
source_text: I've been sober 11 years and I help people find their way in.

name: Hana
description: Spanish and English interpreter providing multilingual access and communication support for the community.
source_text: Hana (interpreter, Spanish/English)

name: Clark-Fulton Moms Network
description: Informal WhatsApp group of ~85 mostly Spanish-speaking members that shares school information, coordinates rides, and distributes safety alerts.
source_text: Clark-Fulton Moms Network | Informal WhatsApp group, ~85 members, mostly Spanish-speaking; shares school info, ride coordination, safety alerts | Rosa

name: St. Michael Parish Men's Group
description: Weekly Thursday meetings that organize and run a food pantry twice monthly.
source_text: St. Michael Parish Men's Group | Weekly Thursday meetings; runs food pantry 2x/month | Father Ignacio

name: Fulton Rd. Block Club (3600 block)
description: Monthly neighborhood group active since 2003 that maintains alleys and plants trees.
source_text: Fulton Rd. Block Club (3600 block) | Monthly; active since 2003; handles alley maintenance, tree planting | Mrs. Cunningham

name: Clark Ave. Merchants Circle
description: Informal association of ~12 storefront owners that meets quarterly to coordinate as local business owners.
source_text: Clark Ave. Merchants Circle | Informal association of ~12 storefront owners; meets quarterly at El Rincon | Sal

name: Clark Ave. Merchants Circle
description: Association of ~12 storefront owners representing active retail presence and local commerce in the neighborhood.
source_text: Clark Ave. Merchants Circle | Informal association of ~12 storefront owners; meets quarterly at El Rincon | Sal

name: Cleveland Youth Boxing Club
description: Youth program with 30–40 regular participants that rents space at the recreation center.
source_text: Cleveland Youth Boxing Club | Rents space at the rec center; 30–40 youth participants | DeShawn

name: Recovery Community
description: Informal word-of-mouth network of residents in recovery who connect newcomers to AA, NA, and housing support.
source_text: Recovery community (informal) | Described as "word of mouth" — residents in recovery who connect newcomers to AA, NA, and housing

name: Intergenerational Knitting Circle
description: Multigenerational group of 8–12 members with mixed backgrounds that meets at the library branch.
source_text: Intergenerational Knitting Circle | Meets at the library branch; 8–12 members; multigenerational, mixed background | Bridget

name: Czech & Slovak Heritage Club
description: Legacy organization of the older generation that maintains cultural continuity through an annual picnic drawing ~60 people.
source_text: Czech & Slovak Heritage Club (legacy) | Noted as less active; older generation; annual picnic at Denison Park still draws ~60 people | Mr. Prochazka

name: Czech & Slovak Heritage Club
description: Legacy organization that maintains Czech and Slovak traditions and heritage through annual community gatherings.
source_text: Czech & Slovak Heritage Club (legacy) | Noted as less active; older generation; annual picnic at Denison Park still draws ~60 people | Mr. Prochazka

name: MetroHealth System
description: Major healthcare institution that employs multiple residents and serves as the primary healthcare provider for uninsured community members.
source_text: MetroHealth System (main campus, W. 25th St.) — Multiple residents work there; also cited as primary healthcare provider for uninsured community members

name: Cleveland Metropolitan School District
description: Public school district serving the neighborhood through Lincoln-West HS and Orchard STEM School.
source_text: Cleveland Metropolitan School District — Lincoln-West HS and Orchard STEM School both named

name: Cleveland Public Library — South Branch
description: Public library providing immigration help, ESL classes, job application assistance, and serving as a community gathering space.
source_text: Cleveland Public Library — South Branch (Fulton & Denison) — Named multiple times. Bridget: "That library is doing more for this neighborhood than people realize. Immigration help, ESL, job applications — all of it."

name: La Sagrada Familia
description: Faith institution hosting community meetings and serving as a gathering place.
source_text: La Sagrada Familia (hosting tonight's session)

name: St. Michael the Archangel Parish
description: Faith institution serving the community through religious services and community programs.
source_text: St. Michael the Archangel Parish

name: New Hope Baptist Church
description: Faith institution running a summer program with free daily lunches that served 200 children last year.
source_text: New Hope Baptist Church (Antoine: "They run a summer program, free lunch every day, 200 kids last year")

name: Pentecostal Church on Clark
description: Faith institution noted as important to the newer Central American community.
source_text: Luz named a Pentecostal church on Clark she could not recall the name of — noted as important to the newer Central American community

name: Cleveland Ward 14 Council Office
description: Government office with mixed community engagement record, responsive on some issues like street lighting.
source_text: Cleveland Ward 14 Council office — mixed responses; Yolanda said they've been responsive on street lighting; Terrell said "only during election years"

name: Cuyahoga County JFS
description: County social services agency providing resources, though noted as difficult to navigate and not always welcoming.
source_text: Cuyahoga County JFS — named as a resource, but with reservations ("hard to navigate," "not always welcoming," multiple voices)

name: Cleveland EMS Station 21
description: Emergency services station with strong relationships and personal connections to longtime residents.
source_text: Cleveland EMS Station 21 on Fulton — Tamara noted strong relationships between the crew and longtime residents; "they know people by name"

name: Denison Park
description: A well-used neighborhood park with basketball courts that serves as a daily social hub and provides recreational opportunities that keep youth engaged, though it has lighting concerns after dark.
source_text: Denison Park — received the most green dots. Jerome: "That park kept me out of trouble. It keeps kids out of trouble now." Basketball courts cited as daily social hub in summer.

name: Brookside Park / Metropark
description: A nearby natural space with aesthetic value, though neighborhood residents feel disconnected from it and may not perceive it as accessible to them.
source_text: Brookside Park / Metropark (nearby) — mentioned as "close but feels disconnected." Kenji: "It's beautiful but people from this neighborhood don't feel like it's for them."

name: Community garden on W. 41st
description: A managed green space behind Orchard school providing garden plots for neighborhood residents and representing one of the most requested assets to expand.
source_text: Community garden on W. 41st (behind Orchard school) — Yolanda manages it; 18 plots; waitlist of 12 households. Named as one of the most requested assets to expand.

name: Fulton Road corridor
description: A commercial street corridor that serves as a hub for neighborhood commerce and daily life, though with changing character over time.
source_text: Fulton Road corridor — mixed response. Long-time residents miss Czech/Slovak storefronts; newer residents value the taquerias, carnicerías, and quinceañera shops.

name: El Rincon Restaurant
description: A restaurant that functions as an informal community gathering place and hub for neighborhood social connection.
source_text: El Rincon Restaurant — named multiple times as a gathering place and informal community hub

name: El Rincon Restaurant
description: A local business that provides employment and economic activity within the neighborhood.
source_text: El Rincon Restaurant — named multiple times as a gathering place and informal community hub

name: Czech Cultural Garden building on W. 38th
description: A vacant historic building that represents underutilized space with potential for community repurposing and cultural programming.
source_text: The old Czech Cultural Garden building on W. 38th — vacant for 3 years; yellow dot. Multiple people said "someone should do something with that."

name: RTA Route 22
description: Public transportation infrastructure that provides critical connectivity for neighborhood residents to jobs and services.
source_text: RTA Route 22 (Clark Ave.) — named as critical; concern about recent schedule cuts

name: Sal's Auto Glass
description: A long-established local business that employs neighborhood residents and demonstrates commitment to the community through local hiring and property stewardship.
source_text: Sal's Auto Glass (Clark Ave.) — Sal noted he employs 4 local residents, all from the neighborhood. Has resisted offers to sell the building: "I've been here 22 years. This is my place."

name: Local restaurants and carnicerías
description: Small food-service businesses that provide employment, particularly for teenagers, and economic activity within the neighborhood.
source_text: Several small restaurants along Fulton — Rosa said the carnicería owner (unnamed, not present) employs teenagers for after-school delivery work

name: Yolanda's catering business
description: A home-based food service business operated by a neighborhood resident, providing employment and economic opportunity.
source_text: Home-based businesses mentioned: catering (Yolanda)

name: Tamara's hair braiding business
description: A home-based personal services business operated by a neighborhood resident, providing employment and economic opportunity.
source_text: Home-based businesses mentioned: catering (Yolanda), hair braiding (Tamara)

name: Neighborhood tax preparation service
description: A home-based professional service operated by a trusted neighborhood resident, providing economic opportunity and knowledge sharing.
source_text: Home-based businesses mentioned: catering (Yolanda), hair braiding (Tamara), tax preparation (unnamed resident, "very trusted," word of mouth only)

name: Small engine repair business
description: A garage-based repair service operated by a neighborhood resident that maintains steady business through word-of-mouth reputation.
location: Two blocks from meeting location, Clark-Fulton
source_text: Jerome mentioned a guy two blocks over who does small engine repair out of his garage and "never advertises but always busy"

name: Neighborhood residents employed at MetroHealth and schools
description: Multiple residents who work at major neighborhood institutions, representing skilled workforce, institutional connections, and economic participation.
source_text: Multiple participants noted that neighborhood residents work at MetroHealth, the schools, and local businesses — but often don't know each other in that capacity.

name: Informally maintained vacant lot on W. 47th
description: A neighborhood green space that residents have collectively cared for and improved through informal stewardship over 5+ years.
source_text: Several vacant lots identified on the map — one on W. 47th that neighbors have informally maintained for 5+ years; Bridget: "We mow it, we planted flowers, we treat it like ours. But it's not ours."

name: Jerome
description: A neighborhood resident with personal experience of parks' positive impact on youth development and community wellbeing.
source_text: Jerome: "That park kept me out of trouble. It keeps kids out of trouble now."

name: Denise
description: A community facilitator who organizes participatory mapping activities and creates space for community dialogue.
source_text: Denise unrolled a large printed street map of Clark-Fulton and placed it on the center table.

name: Rosa
description: A neighborhood resident who represents newer residents' perspectives and experiences of the changing neighborhood economy.
source_text: Rosa, gently: "But it feeds my family now."

name: Kenji
description: A resident who recognizes community assets and opportunities for better connection among neighborhood residents.
source_text: Kenji: "We're all part of the same economy but we don't connect the dots."

name: Mrs. Prochazka
description: A long-time neighborhood resident who carries historical knowledge and cultural memory of the neighborhood's Czech/Slovak heritage.
source_text: Mrs. Prochazka: "I miss what it was."

name: Yolanda
description: A neighborhood resident who manages the community garden, demonstrates environmental stewardship, and operates a home-based business.
source_text: Community garden on W. 41st (behind Orchard school) — Yolanda manages it; 18 plots; waitlist of 12 households.

name: Bridget
description: A neighborhood resident who leads informal community stewardship and environmental care of shared spaces.
source_text: Bridget: "We mow it, we planted flowers, we treat it like ours. But it's not ours."

name: Mrs. Cunningham
description: A long-time neighborhood resident who observes housing changes and their impacts on community connection.
source_text: Mrs. Cunningham noted that 3 homes on her block are now rental-owned by an out-of-town LLC.

name: Darla
description: A neighborhood resident who proposes innovative community economic solutions and recognizes potential for mutual aid.
source_text: Darla raised the idea of a neighborhood skills exchange or time bank — well-received

name: Marcus
description: A community organizer or coordinator tasked with researching and building connections to economic mutual aid infrastructure.
source_text: [ACTION: Research existing time bank / mutual aid infrastructure in Cleveland — Marcus to follow up]

name: Fulton Road commercial corridor storefronts
description: Historic Czech/Slovak storefronts and newer immigrant-owned taquerias and specialty shops that carry cultural identity and traditions.
source_text: Long-time residents miss Czech/Slovak storefronts; newer residents value the taquerias, carnicerías, and quinceañera shops.

name: Mr. Prochazka
description: Community member with deep knowledge of Czech and Slovak heritage, family history of building in the neighborhood, and active organizer of cultural traditions.
source_text: Mr. Prochazka spoke at length about the Czech and Slovak families who built the parish, the butcher shops, the sokol (gymnastic) halls. "My grandfather built two of those buildings with his hands."

name: Mrs. Cunningham
description: Community member who carries family history of Black migration to the neighborhood and can articulate the significance of the area as a historical destination.
source_text: Mrs. Cunningham recalled the neighborhood's history as a destination for Black families during the Great Migration: "My family came from Mississippi in 1951. This block was our foothold."

name: Luz
description: Community member representing Central American families who has cultural knowledge and articulates the contributions of newer immigrant communities.
source_text: Luz described how Central American families, many from Guatemala and Honduras, began arriving in the 1990s and 2000s: "We made this neighborhood ours, too. The food, the church, the quinceañeras — that's culture, that's history."

name: Rosa
description: Community member with knowledge of quinceañera traditions and their role as a neighborhood cultural practice.
source_text: Rosa described it as "like a neighborhood festival, every Saturday in spring and summer. The dresses, the limos, the families — the whole block celebrates."

name: Antoine
description: Community member and long-term organizer of cultural celebrations and community gatherings.
source_text: Antoine: "We've been doing it for 19 years. Last year, 400 people came."

name: DeShawn
description: Community member involved with the boxing club and mentor to young men developing spoken word performance skills.
source_text: DeShawn mentioned that a few young men from the boxing club have started doing spoken word at a coffee shop on W. 25th

name: Hana
description: Community member who recognizes and articulates the value of oral history and immigrant community knowledge-keeping.
source_text: Hana noted that oral history is particularly important in the immigrant community — "people carry whole histories in their memory. They just don't get asked."

name: Terrell
description: Long-time neighborhood resident who represents unconnected community members and potential bridge between cultural communities.
source_text: Terrell said, "I didn't know about the Czech picnic. I've lived two blocks from that park for eight years."

name: Young men from boxing club
description: Youth with emerging spoken word and performance skills who are developing artistic expression.
source_text: a few young men from the boxing club have started doing spoken word at a coffee shop on W. 25th

name: Czech/Slovak Heritage Picnic
description: Annual gathering that organizes and sustains Czech and Slovak cultural traditions and community connection.
source_text: Annual Czech/Slovak Heritage Picnic at Denison Park — Mr. Prochazka; ~60 attendees, homemade kolaches, polka music

name: Quinceañera celebrations on Fulton
description: Informal but consistent community tradition that brings families and neighbors together across the neighborhood.
source_text: Quinceañera season on Fulton — Rosa described it as "like a neighborhood festival, every Saturday in spring and summer. The dresses, the limos, the families — the whole block celebrates."

name: New Hope Baptist Juneteenth Block Party
description: Long-established annual celebration that mobilizes community participation and demonstrates sustained community organizing capacity.
source_text: New Hope Baptist's Juneteenth Block Party — Antoine: "We've been doing it for 19 years. Last year, 400 people came."

name: Día de los Muertos altar at La Sagrada Familia
description: Informal gathering practice that creates cross-cultural participation and honors shared human experiences across communities.
source_text: Informal Día de los Muertos altar at La Sagrada Familia — described as increasingly attended by non-Latino neighbors: "People come who didn't grow up with it. They bring photos of their people too."

name: Boxing club
description: Youth-serving organization that provides space for development of artistic and performance skills.
source_text: a few young men from the boxing club have started doing spoken word at a coffee shop on W. 25th

name: Connector team
description: Newly forming informal group committed to linking community assets and facilitating collaboration across cultural and organizational boundaries.
source_text: who would be willing to be part of a follow-up "connector team" to help link these assets to each other? Eleven hands went up.

name: New Hope Baptist
description: Faith community institution that organizes major annual celebration demonstrating institutional capacity and community leadership.
source_text: New Hope Baptist's Juneteenth Block Party — Antoine: "We've been doing it for 19 years. Last year, 400 people came."

name: La Sagrada Familia
description: Faith community institution that hosts cultural gatherings and demonstrates cross-cultural openness and inclusion.
source_text: Informal Día de los Muertos altar at La Sagrada Familia — described as increasingly attended by non-Latino neighbors

name: MetroHealth community health worker program
description: Health institution with community engagement resources being identified for partnership potential.
source_text: Confirm MetroHealth community health worker program details

name: Orchard STEM
description: Educational institution being identified as potential community partnership resource.
source_text: Reach out to Orchard STEM principal re: community partnership

name: Pentecostal church
description: Faith community institution representing untapped institutional asset being invited to community engagement.
source_text: Invite unnamed Pentecostal church to next session

name: Denison Park
description: Public green space that hosts cultural celebrations and serves as gathering place for community traditions.
source_text: Annual Czech/Slovak Heritage Picnic at Denison Park

name: Coffee shop on W. 25th
description: Public business space that provides venue for emerging artistic expression and youth performance.
source_text: a few young men from the boxing club have started doing spoken word at a coffee shop on W. 25th

name: Fulton Street
description: Neighborhood street that serves as site of recurring cultural celebrations and community visibility.
source_text: Quinceañera season on Fulton

name: Vacant lot on W. 47th
description: Unused land identified as potential asset for community development if ownership can be determined.
source_text: Identify owner of vacant lot on W. 47th — Cuyahoga County land records

name: El Rincon
description: Local business that supports community cultural expression and hosts public art.
source_text: Mural on the side of El Rincon (painted 2019 by a local artist, name not recalled)

name: Local butcher shops
description: Historical neighborhood businesses that were central to Czech and Slovak community economic life and identity.
source_text: Mr. Prochazka spoke at length about the Czech and Slovak families who built the parish, the butcher shops, the sokol (gymnastic) halls.

name: Czech and Slovak heritage — family history
description: Living cultural heritage carried by community members and expressed through language, building traditions, and family memory.
source_text: Mr. Prochazka spoke at length about the Czech and Slovak families who built the parish, the butcher shops, the sokol (gymnastic) halls. "My grandfather built two of those buildings with his hands. That's not gone — it's in the walls."

name: Black migration history and family legacy
description: Living cultural memory and identity rooted in Great Migration history and family connections to place.
source_text: Mrs. Cunningham recalled the neighborhood's history as a destination for Black families during the Great Migration: "My family came from Mississippi in 1951. This block was our foothold."

name: Central American culture and traditions
description: Living cultural heritage of Guatemalan and Honduran families expressed through food, religion, and celebration traditions.
source_text: Luz described how Central American families, many from Guatemala and Honduras, began arriving in the 1990s and 2000s: "We made this neighborhood ours, too. The food, the church, the quinceañeras — that's culture, that's history."

name: Czech/Slovak Heritage Picnic
description: Annual celebration that transmits Czech and Slovak cultural traditions including food, music, and community identity.
source_text: Annual Czech/Slovak Heritage Picnic at Denison Park — Mr. Prochazka; ~60 attendees, homemade kolaches, polka music

name: Quinceañera tradition
description: Living cultural tradition marking life passage and family identity that is visible and shared across the community.
source_text: Quinceañera season on Fulton — Rosa described it as "like a neighborhood festival, every Saturday in spring and summer. The dresses, the limos, the families — the whole block celebrates."

name: Juneteenth commemoration
description: Annual celebration that transmits historical knowledge and cultural memory of Black liberation and community identity.
source_text: New Hope Baptist's Juneteenth Block Party — Antoine: "We've been doing it for 19 years. Last year, 400 people came."

name: Día de los Muertos tradition
description: Living cultural practice that honors deceased loved ones and creates cross-cultural spaces for remembrance and belonging.
source_text: Informal Día de los Muertos altar at La Sagrada Familia — described as increasingly attended by non-Latino neighbors: "People come who didn't grow up with it. They bring photos of their people too."

name: Neighborhood history mural
description: Visual art that depicts and celebrates neighborhood history across multiple cultural communities.
source_text: Mural on the side of El Rincon (painted 2019 by a local artist, name not recalled) — multiple people mentioned it; described as showing neighborhood history across cultures

name: Spoken word and performance
description: Emerging artistic and literary tradition being developed by young people to express identity and experience.
source_text: a few young men from the boxing club have started doing spoken word at a coffee shop on W. 25th — "it started as something to do after sparring. Now they perform."

name: Oral history tradition
description: Living practice of memory-keeping and knowledge transmission particularly important in immigrant communities.
source_text: Hana noted that oral history is particularly important in the immigrant community — "people carry whole histories in their memory. They just don't get asked."

name: Sokol (gymnastic) halls
description: Historical institutions that preserved Czech and Slovak cultural identity and community gathering traditions.
source_text: Mr. Prochazka spoke at length about the Czech and Slovak families who built the parish, the butcher shops, the sokol (gymnastic) halls.

name: Marcus Webb
description: Community facilitator with capacity to compile and synthesize meeting notes and asset information.
source_text: Notes compiled by Marcus Webb.

name: Denise Okafor
description: Community member with review capacity and editorial oversight for community development documentation.
source_text: Draft circulated to Denise Okafor for review on April 11, 2025.

name: Terrell
description: Community member identified as a potential cross-cultural connection facilitator through relational bridge-building.
source_text: Key relational bridge: Terrell/Mr. Prochazka exchange — potential for cross-cultural connection facilitation

name: Mr. Prochazka
description: Community member identified as a potential cross-cultural connection facilitator through relational bridge-building.
source_text: Key relational bridge: Terrell/Mr. Prochazka exchange — potential for cross-cultural connection facilitation

name: Clark-Fulton Community Development Collaborative
description: Collaborative group overseeing community development work and governance of shared information and initiatives.
source_text: Not for external distribution without review by the Clark-Fulton Community Development Collaborative.

name: Cultural assets
description: Community traditions, expressions, and heritage identified as the strongest energy present in the community.
source_text: Strongest energy: Cultural assets section

name: Home-based and informal economy
description: Local economic activity and income-generation occurring through informal and home-based work, identified as underexplored.
source_text: Most underexplored: Economic assets — particularly home-based and informal economy

name: JFS
description: Formal organization identified as having institutional trust gap that will shape how future initiatives are structured.
source_text: Institutional trust gap (JFS, Ward office) will shape how any future initiatives are structured

name: Ward office
description: Government institution identified as having institutional trust gap that will shape how future initiatives are structured.
source_text: Institutional trust gap (JFS, Ward office) will shape how any future initiatives are structured
